business.summary: Infosys and Wipro ADRs plunged following Accenture’s reduced FY26 revenue forecast. This downgrade highlights enterprise caution regarding discretionary IT spending, impacting Indian IT giants despite their AI investments and ongoing transformation projects, overshadowing recent cybersecurity acquisitions.

business.title: Sebi proposes easing margin trading funding rules, tighter broker norms
business.summary: SEBI has proposed reforms to the Margin Trading Facility, including increasing broker net-worth requirements to Rs 5 crore, permitting LLPs to offer MTF, and expanding funding avenues. These changes aim to enhance operational efficiency and strengthen risk management for brokers.

business.summary: Wall Street's major indexes saw gains on Thursday, with technology shares spearheading the advance. Optimism surrounding a potential Middle East peace deal helped to overshadow concerns about a hawkish Federal Reserve under new Chair Kevin Warsh. The Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq all opened higher.

business.title: West Asia conflict, El Niño threat to hit India Inc. earnings in Q1, says ICRA
business.summary: ICRA warned that the combination of higher crude oil prices and rupee depreciation could fuel inflationary pressures and constrain consumption-led sectors.India Inc. is likely to face slower revenue growth and weaker profitability in the first quarter of FY27 as the West Asia conflict, rising crude oil prices, rupee depreciation and the risk of an El Niño-induced demand slowdown create fresh headwinds for corporate earnings, according to rating agency ICRA.

business.title: Christy Mathai used market dip to buy insurance, logistics stocks; here's his sector-by-sector playbook
business.summary: Fund Manager Christy Mathai sees easing war-driven inflation fears, expecting a shorter earnings hit. He's selectively adding to his portfolio, favoring financials and IT over defense and chemicals. Mathai anticipates an AI-driven inflection point for IT services and sees near-term tailwinds for FMCG, while adopting a stock-specific approach to pharma.

business.title: 37 per cent of entry-level tasks in India already done by AI, finds Cognizant and Pearson Study

business.title: Quote of the day by Charles Ellis: "There’s an old market saying about stocks to the effect that they all go down together"
business.summary: Charles Ellis highlights how stocks often decline together during market stress, challenging the perceived safety of diversification. The piece explains how investor sentiment drives correlations higher in crises and why long-term investors should remain disciplined, as such phases are temporary and often followed by recovery led by fundamentally strong companies.

business.summary: Under the smart warehousing system, CWC is deploying advanced AI and IoT-driven technologies in foodgrain and general warehouses

business.title: India bond rally defies hawkish Fed on oil rout
business.summary: Indian government bonds are rallying for a sixth day. Falling oil prices are boosting investor confidence. This is happening despite a hawkish outlook from the US Federal Reserve. Expectations of a reopened Strait of Hormuz are driving oil prices down. Cheaper crude oil improves India's inflation and fiscal outlook. Foreign investors are also pouring money into Indian debt.

business.title: Sebi board to consider reintroducing open-market window for buybacks on Friday
business.summary: This Friday, Sebi's board is gearing up for a pivotal meeting focused on several transformative proposals. Among these is the revival of open-market share buybacks, now with a quicker turnaround. Furthermore, the introduction of GARUDA—an innovative green-channel mechanism—looks to streamline the launch process for Alternative Investment Funds.

business.title: UK Pound hits two-month low as dollar surges and BoE holds rates
business.summary: The British pound has fallen to its lowest point in two months. This happened as the US dollar strengthened due to expectations of interest rate hikes in America. The Bank of England decided to keep borrowing costs unchanged. This cautious approach by the Bank of England contrasts with recent moves by other central banks.

business.title: TN Govt not in favour of greenfield airport at Parandur; all options to be explored: Minister

business.title: Bank of England votes 7-2 to hold rates at 3.75%
business.summary: The Bank of England has chosen to maintain interest rates at 3.75 percent, a decision that aligns with prevailing predictions among economists. Although consensus prevailed, a couple of policymakers pushed for a higher rate. As Governor Andrew Bailey notes, inflationary pressures are expected to rise, despite the prospect of a new agreement with Iran.

technology.title: Xiaomi Wants a Bigger Space in Your Home: Confirms Entry Into the Large Appliances Category in India
technology.summary: Xiaomi India has confirmed it will launch large appliances in the second half of 2026. The senior leadership team at Xiaomi India, while addressing a small section of the media, announced the news. At this point, there's no update on which product will launch first, but hinted that local testing is already on. The company also touched upon localising manufacturing, and this could mean that we may see Made-in-India large appliances with the Xiaomi logo in India before the end of 2026.

technology.title: Xiaomi India CMO Anuj Sharma Steps Down After 8 Years
technology.summary: Anuj Sharma, CMO, Xiaomi India, has stepped down from the role after nearly eight years. Sharma also joked about how the absolute pressure cooker of the Indian smartphone space over the last eight years has physically aged him.
